feedback.text: Dear Members of the Digital Fairness Act Committee, I share the concerns raised by the StopKillingGames initiative regarding digital ownership, particularly in gaming. Purchasing a digital game often means receiving a revocable license, not true ownership, access can be lost due to server shutdowns, policy changes, or lack of offline support. This is unfair, especially when paying full price. Digital products should offer lasting access, just like physical ones. We need stronger consumer protections to ensure continued access and offline functionality wherever technically possible. Thank you for your attention.
